mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, directly increasing the present value of expected corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with rate declines.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points over a three-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances, or the signal's out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and rising consumer price inflation indicates robust aggregate demand outpacing supply constraints, forcing equity markets to price in higher corporate margins and yielding positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive rank IC over 60-day horizons, or returns are negative when both series trend upward simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals improving manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which triggers short-term momentum buying in equity markets and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in over 60% of instances when industrial production accelerates, or the signal loses predictive power outside recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC remains below 0.01, or payroll growth shows no significant positive correlation with 20-day equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.5 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 70% of high-inflation trigger months, or the signal's predictive accuracy falls below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and housing starts indicates synchronized strength across broad economic output and interest-rate-sensitive sectors, which reinforces earnings upgrade cycles and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative rank IC over 60-day horizons, or equity returns underperform when both GDP and housing starts trend upward.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment alongside a stable or declining federal funds rate signals a liquidity trap or deflationary recession risk, which destroys equity valuations through collapsing earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in over 65% of instances when unemployment rises with stable rates, or the signal fails to predict downturns in historical recessionary periods.
